LAW AND MOTION, DEPT 02, MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES, CALENDAR OF OCT-28-1996, DENIED THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY. CCP 2024. THE COURT FINDS THE MOTION SHOULD HAVE BEEN MADE BY 10/7/96 WITHOUT A MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY HAVING BEEN GRANTED OR THERE BEING A STIPULATION/ AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT CROSSGROVE, CARL BROWN, RICK ON THE COMPLAINT, SAID DEFENDANT TO HAVE JUDGMENT THEREON FOR $476.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $476.00

CCP 2024(F) SPECIFICALLY STATES ".. REOPEN DISCOVERY AFTER A NEW DATE FOR TRIAL... " IT IS CLEAR FROM CCP 2024(E) & (F) THAT DISCOVERY CLOSES 5 DAYS BEFORE ORIGINAL TRIAL DATE; OTHERWISE THE LANGUAGE RELATING TO MOTION OR AGREEMENT....
